name: Lethe River
description: The Lethe River, also known as the River of Forgetfulness, is one of the five rivers of the Greek Underworld, Hades. Souls who drank from the Lethe River forgot their past lives, allowing them to be reborn without the memories and burdens of their previous existence. This oblivion was considered essential for the cycle of reincarnation. The river itself was not a physical place accessible to the living; instead, it represented a symbolic boundary and a necessary stage in the transition between life and death. The act of drinking from the Lethe ensured that the newly reborn soul would not be haunted or influenced by their former selves. The Lethe River is often depicted as a dark, slow-moving body of water, embodying the stillness and silence of oblivion. In some accounts, not all souls were required to drink from it; only those destined for reincarnation partook of its waters. The river's power was absolute, erasing all memories, experiences, and even the sense of self. The concept of Lethe has become a powerful metaphor for forgetting, oblivion, and the erasure of the past, influencing literature, art, and psychology for centuries.
subtitle: River of Forgetfulness in Hades

keyMyths.title: Drinking from Lethe
keyMyths.description: Souls destined for rebirth drink from the Lethe River to forget their past lives. This ensures a fresh start without the burdens of their previous existence.

keyMyths.title: Virgil's Aeneid
keyMyths.description: In Virgil's Aeneid, Aeneas visits the Underworld and witnesses souls drinking from the Lethe River, preparing for their return to the world of the living.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Mentions the Underworld, though not specifically Lethe.
sources.text: Mentions the Underworld, though not specifically Lethe.

sources.author: Plato
sources.description: Discusses the concept of souls drinking from the river of forgetfulness.
sources.text: Discusses the concept of souls drinking from the river of forgetfulness.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: Depicts Aeneas witnessing souls at the Lethe River.
